untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detecting divergence between two correlated time series where one reverses while the other continues, and measuring divergence magnitude. The specific mechanics of (1) establishing prior correlation, (2) detecting which series reverses first, (3) measuring divergence as a percentage of recent range, and (4) confirming on first bar of reversal cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The signal requires custom multi-series comparison logic not supported by point-in-time indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: This is a pair-trading divergence signal requiring real-time correlation analysis and relative momentum tracking between two time series. It fundamentally requires custom backtesting logic beyond indicator thresholds. The 8-bar trend establishment, 0.5% magnitude threshold, and first-bar detection pattern cannot be parameterized with standard indicator operators.
